subject: [PATCH v3 02/12] audit: widen ino fields to u64

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the use of u32 instead of u64 in the audit_hash_ino function, which could lead to incorrect hash values on 32-bit platforms. The author agrees that this is an issue and will fix it by casting the u64 value to u32 before performing the bitwise AND operation.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch widens inode number fields in the Linux audit subsystem from unsigned long to u64 to prevent truncation of inode numbers on 32-bit platforms, which can cause incorrect audit log entries and broken watch/mark comparisons.
